acquisition method: Mass spectrometric identification was performed using the Data-Independent Acquisition (DIA) strategy.
cleavage agent: trypsin was specified as the digestive enzyme with a maximum of 1 missed cleavage allowed
collision energy: the collision energy was set at 25 eV (higher-energy collisional dissociation, HCD)
fractionation method: the elution fraction range for size exclusion separation of Fo-EVs was determined based on the relative fluorescence intensity (RFU) and protein concentration at each fraction.
fragmentation method: the collision energy was set at 25 eV (higher-energy collisional dissociation, HCD)
instrument: the peptides were directly introduced into the Astral High-Resolution Mass Spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Shanghai, China) for DIA detection.
ionization type: Mass spectrometric data were acquired in positive ion mode with a spray voltage of 2000 V
labeling: mass spectrometry analysis of EV protein samples was conducted by Guangke Ande Co., Ltd.
